identity.short_name: Sharp constant via uvw+SOS on a degree-4 symmetric form

problem_spec.summary: Under $a+b+c=1$, determine the sharp constant $K$ in a symmetric homogeneous degree-4 inequality and output $(p+q)\bmod 1000$ for $K=p/q$.
givens: Fixed coefficient $\lambda=\frac{132}{35}$ in the target form.
Define $K=\frac{p}{q}$ in lowest terms and $N\in\{0,\dots,999\}$ by $N\equiv p+q\pmod{1000}$.
ask: Find the largest real constant $K$ such that $(a^2+b^2+c^2)^2+\lambda abc(a+b+c)\ge K(a+b+c)^4$ holds for all $a,b,c>0$, and characterize all equality cases.
Compute the integer output $N$ defined above.

parameter(s).name: constraint_model
parameter(s).purpose: Select the normalization used by normalize.

parameter(s).name: degree_d
parameter(s).purpose: Controls the total degree after homogenize and thus the SOS/uvw complexity.

parameter(s).name: coefficient_profile
parameter(s).purpose: Varies the symmetric polynomial form $F$ while keeping the same SOS/uvw skeleton.

invariant(s).name: symmetric_homogeneous_form
invariant(s).statement: After homogenize and normalize, the task becomes a three-variable symmetric homogeneous polynomial inequality of total degree $d$ on a compact normalization slice, and the sharp constant is a minimum value.

invariant(s).name: uvw_reduction_validity
invariant(s).statement: For fixed $u=a+b+c$ and $v=ab+bc+ca$, the target expression is affine in $w=abc$ (degree $4$ case), enabling reduction to the two-equal boundary.

invariant(s).name: equality_locus
invariant(s).statement: Equality is attained on the $b=c$ boundary at the minimizer of the reduced one-variable polynomial, and by symmetry its permutations give all equality cases.

invariant(s).name: interface_quantity_T
invariant(s).statement: Define $T=(u,v)$ where $u=a+b+c$ and $v=ab+bc+ca$ after normalize; uvw treats $T$ as fixed and reduces extremality in $w$ to boundary cases.

essential_assumption(s): The normalization constraint makes the feasible set bounded (and its closure compact), so the sharp constant is well-defined as a minimum on the closure.
Coefficient_profile excludes trivial factorization or loss of symmetry.
uniqueness_certificate(s): uvw_reduction makes the gap polynomial affine in $w$, so the minimum occurs at boundary configurations with two equal variables; the sharp $K$ is forced by a double-root (tangency) condition in the one-variable reduction and is certified by a factor/SOS decomposition.

technique(s).name: normalize
technique(s).purpose: Fix scale (here $a+b+c=1$) to reduce degrees of freedom and expose polynomial structure.
outputs: compactified feasible slice and a minimum-value interpretation of $K$

technique(s).name: uvw_reduction
inputs: symmetric polynomial inequality in three variables
technique(s).purpose: Reduce to a one-variable extremal problem by fixing $(u,v)$ and using affinity in $w$.

technique(s).name: SOS
technique(s).purpose: Provide an explicit nonnegativity certificate via factorization and discriminant/complete-square.

technique(s).name: substitution
technique(s).purpose: Convert the symmetric inequality into a single-variable rational/polynomial inequality.

technique(s).name: extremal_principle
inputs: continuous symmetric function on a normalized compact closure
technique(s).purpose: Guarantee an extremum exists and justify searching on uvw boundary cases.

technique(s).name: univariate_critical_point_analysis
technique(s).purpose: Locate the unique global minimizer by factoring $g'(t)$ and performing a sign chart.

theorem(s).name: AM_GM
theorem(s).role: Provide baseline inequality $\frac{a}{b}+\frac{b}{c}+\frac{c}{a}\ge 3$ and identify symmetry-equality conditions.

theorem(s).name: Cauchy_Schwarz
theorem(s).role: Relate $u,v,w$ via $(ab+bc+ca)^2\ge 3abc(a+b+c)$ and rewrite symmetric squares in $(u,v)$.

theorem(s).name: Schur
theorem(s).role: Give a canonical inequality $u^3+9w\ge 4uv$ used to constrain $w$ in uvw arguments.

theorem(s).name: quadratic_discriminant_test
theorem(s).role: Conclude a quadratic is positive for all real $t$ from negative discriminant and positive leading coefficient.

critical_trick(s): Normalize by $a+b+c=1$ and rewrite the gap polynomial in $(u,v,w)$.
Use uvw affinity in $w$ to restrict to the two-equal boundary.
Solve the one-variable minimization exactly and certify by factor/SOS.

library(s).name: sympy
library(s).reason: Exact rational arithmetic, symbolic differentiation, and factorization of the reduced polynomial gap.

library(s).name: random
library(s).reason: Independent numerical sampling on the simplex $a+b+c=1$ to stress-test the inequality.

library(s).name: math
library(s).reason: Floating evaluation and gcd/mod computations for the final normalization check.

task(s).name: primary_structural_check
task(s).reason: Symbolically substitute $b=c=1,a=t$ and factor the gap polynomial to confirm the SOS-style factorization and positivity of the remaining quadratic (via discriminant).

task(s).name: secondary_independent_check
task(s).reason: Randomly sample many triples on $a+b+c=1$ and numerically verify the inequality; separately scan the two-equal curve $(1-2x,x,x)$ to locate the minimum near $(1/15,7/15,7/15)$ and confirm sharpness.
